title: Average Web Page Size 2026: How Heavy Are Top Websites?
description: Original research: Average web page weight is 2.2 MB across 100 top websites. JavaScript accounts for the largest share.

schemas.headline: Average Web Page Size 2026: HTML, CSS, JS Breakdown of 100 Top Websites
schemas.description: Original research measuring full page weight across 100 top websites including HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and image sizes

mainEntity.name: What is the average web page size in 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: Based on our measurement of 100 top websites, the average web page size is 2.2 MB (2,217 KB). This includes the HTML document, all CSS stylesheets, JavaScript files, and discoverable images. The median is 1.5 MB.

mainEntity.name: How much JavaScript does the average website load?

acceptedAnswer.text: JavaScript is the largest component of most web pages. In our study, sites like YouTube load 9.5 MB of JavaScript alone, and Discord loads 6.9 MB. JavaScript typically accounts for 60-80% of a page's total weight.
YouTube (12.9 MB), Discord (9.9 MB), and GitHub (9.0 MB) are the heaviest sites in our study. These are JavaScript-heavy web applications that load significant client-side code.
Simple marketing and corporate sites tend to be lightest. Oracle.com measured just 3 KB, and several sites with heavy CDN optimization came in under 200 KB for their initial page load.

Page weight is the total bytes downloaded to render a page, including the HTML document, all linked CSS stylesheets, JavaScript files, images, and fonts. We measured by fetching the HTML, parsing it for resource URLs, then making HEAD requests to each resource for its Content-Length.
